retrospective.harder_than_needed: Chakra UI's <Modal> component does not forward data-testid to any rendered DOM node — it's a portal wrapper, and the actual DOM element is <ModalContent>. I placed data-testid="block-confirm-modal" on <Modal> itself (mirroring what looked like the established LeaveGroupButton.tsx precedent), and the e2e test failed with a real, unambiguous timeout even though the modal was genuinely open (confirmed via the Playwright error-context snapshot showing the actual `dialog "Block contact?"` node). Moved the testid to <ModalContent> and it passed immediately. Grepping the whole e2e suite afterward showed LeaveGroupButton.tsx's own `leave-group-modal` testid has the identical bug and is never queried by any existing spec — meaning that precedent was itself unverified dead code, not a proven pattern, which is what actually made this harder than it should have been (the 'established' pattern I was mirroring was already broken).
retrospective.surprised_by: The 25 pre-existing baseline test failures (ThemeIcon.test.ts / themes/treatments.test.ts, an icon-set naming mismatch from an apparently concurrent, unrelated theming refactor) required a judgment call to proceed past Step 0's stated zero-tolerance policy — I treated them as pre-existing debt (unrelated files, confirmed via project memory precedent for judging by whether a change's own specs pass rather than the full gate) rather than a BASELINE_BLOCKED halt, since blocking an otherwise well-scoped, fully-specified story over an unrelated in-flight epic's icon-set churn would not have served the actual task.

accepted_residuals: Test-rigor remediation round: extracted shouldIngestDmFromSender (behavior-preserving) + real persist-spy tests for AC-VIEW-8..11; publish-spy now covers block AND unblock; dangling dm-block-privacy.spec.ts refs fixed.
out_of_scope_decisions: Blocked contact can still ring (calls not block-gated): user chose backlog finding (gate-incoming-calls-with-block-deny) + code comment; calls disabled now.
